reasoning.typography: Generic sans-serif with no typographic character — clean but utterly forgettable, typical DTC product site font choices with no discipline or identity
reasoning.hierarchy: Hero headline does visual work, but below-the-fold the three feature circles are identically weighted with no dominance — everything is medium-sized and equal
reasoning.distinctiveness: The headline 'device they'll actually put down' has specific POV, and the product photography shows actual distinctive hardware — not completely swappable, though the structure is still 'hero/features/CTA'
reasoning.color: Blue gradient dominates at ~40% of hero, mint green accent buttons, but the palette lacks discipline — hero is decorative rather than strategic, accent usage exceeds 5% significantly
reasoning.density: Uniform padding throughout, safe whitespace that never takes risks — features section has identical circular frames at identical sizes with identical spacing, no density variation
reasoning.voice: Has some personality in copy ('device they'll actually put down' is specific), but IA is pure stencil: hero product shot + 3-feature grid + trust badges + CTA — structure doesn't reflect the parenting anxiety story

winning_patterns: Headline has actual POV about smartphone addiction and parenting
Product photography shows distinctive physical hardware that couldn't be mistaken for another brand
Circular feature illustrations have playful, kid-friendly curves that match product personality
Copy voice is conversational and specific ('trusted adults into the loop')
anti_patterns: Three equal-weighted feature circles with identical visual treatment — no hierarchy or emphasis
Generic blue gradient hero background is 2019 SaaS aesthetic, not distinctive
Mint green CTA buttons overused — accent color appears at >20% rather than restrained 5%
Trust badge parade at bottom (Times, BBC, EduEvidence) is pure stencil credibility theater
IA is paint-by-numbers: hero → features → CTA → social proof, ignores the emotional parenting journey
Uniform padding/spacing throughout — no confident density or breathing room variation
